step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the summary provided. In myocardial infarction, extensive cellular damage occurs due to prolonged ischemia, resulting in the inability of the remaining intact but dysfunctional myocardial cells to reduce TTC to form azan. Therefore, the affected myocardium will remain uncolored and hence appear white against the normally dyed red healthy myocardium.
